blocks.content: Shirone followed Adel into the mansion.
A lavish hall adorned with numerous art pieces greeted him.
On both sides, spiraled staircases wound their way up to the fourth floor.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Aaah! Amazing! A real man!
A woman, identical to Amy, slapped her cheeks and exclaimed.
It was Isis, the lady of the house.
Despite being in her fifties, the former kingdom-renowned dancer still looked youthful.
However, perhaps because of that, her every move seemed to lack maturity.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: What? Shirone is a man?

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Me too! I want to see!
Two men rushed from the corridor connected to the living room.
They were the eldest son, Daian, and the second son, Ares.
In his mid-thirties, Daian looked surprisingly young, and Ares, with a lush beard, had an indiscernible face.
The two stared at Shirone in disbelief.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Come here, quickly. Don't bother with greetings. Honey, Amy's boyfriend is here.
Isis, undeterred, grabbed Shirone's wrist and sat him on the sofa.
Waiting there was a man Shirone knew by name - Shakora, the head of the Karmis family.
A man of great influence in politics, he had retired, disillusioned with various corruptions.
Hmm.
As Shakora lowered his newspaper, his intellectual appearance was revealed.
He was the tallest in the family, and despite his age, his physique was perfectly balanced.
Shirone sweated coldly, feeling like a rabbit in a lion's den.
The scrutinizing gaze, as if appraising an artwork, was unbearably overwhelming.
The men's eyes glowed red, causing Shirone to swallow nervously.
The sight of the three simultaneously activating their Red Eyes was truly terrifying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh, they remember me. They're storing me in their minds.
Aware of their ability to recall self-images, Shirone felt as if shackled.
They could likely recall this moment anytime, transcending time and space.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Um, hello?
Unable to endure the awkward silence, Shirone, feeling embarrassed, greeted them again.
At that, the men's Red Eyes faded, and Isis, as if waiting for this moment, clung to him cheerfully.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Wow, so handsome. Is this for real? Are you really Amy's friend?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, that's right.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Oh? So you're in the advanced class too?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I am in Class Five.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Hmm?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But I will be in Class Four next semester.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Ah ha!
Suddenly, all three burst into cheerful laughter.
The same expressions, the same reactions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where am I? Who are these people…
Shirone suddenly remembered Adel's words.
- Expect the unexpected. Don't take it to heart.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: When did you meet Amy? You're dating, right? Huh? Do you hold hands at school too?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh? No, that's not...
Shirone stalled for time.
Here, Amy's opinion mattered most, so it was best to be reserved.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What's all the fuss about? Why are you gathering around without the person involved?
Amy's voice descended from the upper floor, saving Shirone, who was on the verge of collapse.
She was looking down from the spiral staircase on the second floor.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Oh? You changed clothes? When Shirone arrived, you ran to your room in a frenzy.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: So what? Should I meet him in my pajamas? Anyway, disperse everyone. Dad, is it okay if I talk with my friend?

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: Sure, why not? Isn't he the first boyfriend you've brought home? Do whatever you need to, so you won't regret it later. That's the Karmis way. Hahaha!
Even with Shakora's teasing joke, Amy didn't bat an eyelid.
It was crucial not to give them an opening, as they'd relentlessly dig deeper.
As Shirone ascended the spiral staircase, Isis continued her questioning.
Aaah! Amazing! A real man!

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Amy, tell me the truth. He's your boyfriend, right? You're dating, right? Huh?
Amy hurried her steps, ignoring the voice.
But her mother, who had passed down her blood, was just as persistent.
Ugh!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ugh!
Unable to hold back, Amy yelled sharply and headed to her room.
Isis stood with her mouth agape.
Even Shakora seemed surprised, looking up the staircase.
But by then, the two had already disappeared from sight.
Entering the room, Shirone and Amy sat awkwardly across a table.
Shirone first surveyed the room.
The library was filled with magic books, and in a corner out of sight were a few unread romance novels.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! Those must be from Senior Sharelle.
At school, she was known as a tough woman, but her room was surprisingly feminine.
Especially the large stuffed animal on the bed caught his eye – a fierce-looking bear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's a stuffed animal. And it's a bear.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: So what? Can't I have a stuffed animal in my room?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I just thought it looked like you. Haha!
Amy clicked her tongue and closed her mouth.
Honestly, she was at a loss for words.
It was the first time she had shown her room to a man other than her family.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But are you really okay? We might pretend to be dating at school, but there's no need to lie to your family.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't worry about it. They probably won't care anyway. They're just having fun with it.
True to Amy's words, her family was free-spirited and unique.
However, Shirone could faintly sense that they were connected by a hidden family love.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Enough with the nonsense. Why suddenly visit my house? Do you have something to say?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Actually, that's...
As Shirone started to speak, the door slid open.
Isis's face sneaked in through the narrow gap.
Oh dear, did I interrupt something? You should have some tea while talking.
Isis gracefully moved to the table and set down the tea, her eyes fixated on Shirone.
As Shirone tried to speak again, the door opened once more, this time revealing Daian.
I brought some snacks...
Daian's gaze also lingered on Shirone.
Walking forward while his head was turned sideways made for a comical scene.
Amy sighed.
Such a thing had never happened before, so it must have been amusing for them.
Her brothers, usually indifferent to anything but extraordinary events, had never had her boyfriend visit the house.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Fine. Everyone come in. I know you're all waiting outside.
Upon Amy's command, feet pushed the door open.
Ares stood there, holding a plate of fruit.
There were some fruits... Haha.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Come in already. Might as well introduce him properly now. Shirone, is that okay?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, of course.
And so, the Karmis family, except Shakora, all sat opposite Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: This is Shirone, a promising student from the Alpheas Magic Academy. And Shirone, this is my mother. She was a dancer in her youth.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: I see. No wonder she's so beautiful.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Hohoho! Amy takes after me. But don't fall for me. I don't want to hurt my daughter.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, yes.
Shirone nodded in agreement.
There really was nothing else he could say.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: And this is my older brother, Daian. He's a Grade-5 Swordsman and commands the archer unit in the capital. He's here to consult Dad on some political matters.
As Amy finished, the family looked at her surprisingly.
Even for a boyfriend, there was no need to mention internal family matters.
It was proof that she, too, was inwardly tense.
